summary.title: Mega Travel Threat Model
summary.description:
MegaTravel is a multinational travel services platform serving over 100 million clients worldwide. The system enables customers to search, plan, and book travel packages including accommodation, transport, and leisure activities via a public web application and mobile app.
The system exposes a public-facing web application and REST API consumed by clients and mobile apps. Internally, it consists of an application server, a central database storing customer PII, payment data, and reservations, and an authentication service managing sessions and access control. An admin portal provides privileged access for employees and system administrators, while an intranet portal supports internal staff operations. The system integrates with external partners (hotels, airlines, car rentals) via a Partner API, and connects to third-party services including payment gateways, email/SMS providers, and geolocation APIs. Remote access for IT and DevOps staff is provided via VPN. The platform operates across three headquarters (London, Boston, Hong Kong) and dozens of branch offices globally.

threats.title: S2.1 - Malware simulates keyboard actions
threats.description: Malver na klijentskom uređaju simulira korisnikove akcije.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: S2.2 - Malware simulates client sending packets
threats.description: Malver šalje pakete u ime korisnika.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: S2.3 - Malware fakes user operation
threats.description: Malver lažira korisničku operaciju na višem nivou.
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: S1.1 - Illegally obtained certificate
threats.description: Legal certificates obtained by the attacker or forged certificate.

threats.title: T1 - Client is embedded malicious program
threats.description: Malware modifies browser memory or tampered with user input.
threats.mitigation: Implement client-side environment hardening and integrity checks.

threats.title: I2 - Sensitive information without secure handling
threats.description: Sensitive information stored locally leading to information leakage.
threats.mitigation: Ensure no sensitive data is cached or stored locally without encryption.

threats.title: E1 - Client security vulnerabilities
threats.description: Client components have security vulnerabilities, leading to a remote attack.
threats.mitigation: Keep client OS and browser components updated.

threats.title: E - Internal request tampering
threats.description: Altering the request payload internally to bypass authorization checks.
threats.mitigation: Digitally sign internal requests or use secure internal tokens (e.g., internal JWTs).

threats.title: R1.2 - Log record flow disruption
threats.description: If an attacker drops or disrupts this specific data flow, it results in missing log records.
threats.mitigation: Implement guaranteed delivery mechanisms for audit logs.

threats.title: I4 - Security mechanism is not perfect
threats.description: Lack of channel security (I4.3) or weak message security (I4.2) across this boundary allows attackers to intercept data.
threats.mitigation: Enforce strong encryption protocols (TLS 1.3) across the boundary.

threats.title: S1.3 / S1.4 - Password Security & Brute force
threats.description: Attackers guessing or brute-forcing the login parameters.
threats.mitigation: Implement rate limiting, account lockout, and strong password policies.

threats.title: T1.1 / T1.4 - Malware tampered request
threats.description: Malware tampered the user request data before it reaches the server.
threats.mitigation: Use mutual TLS and payload signing.

threats.title: I1 / I3 - Interception of login parameters
threats.description: Malware stealing sensitive info or fake site tricking the user (Phishing).
threats.mitigation: Use secure transport (HTTPS), anti-phishing measures, and multi-factor authentication.

threats.title: D1 - Channel overload
threats.description: Abnormal login parameters leading to high CPU/memory consumption.
threats.mitigation: Implement WAF and strict input validation/size limits.

threats.title: S1.2.3 - Server's authentication vulnerability
threats.description: Server’s authentication vulnerability, which can be bypassed.
threats.mitigation: Secure code review and robust authentication libraries.

threats.title: S1.5 - Session mechanism is not perfect
threats.description: Lack of session timeout mechanism or state examination.
threats.mitigation: Implement strict session timeouts and validation.

threats.title: D1.2 / D1.3 - Server Business Hang or Crash
threats.description: Abnormal parameters or multiple concurrent operations cause the server to hang or crash.
threats.mitigation: Resource throttling and proper error handling.

threats.title: E2 - Server security vulnerabilities
threats.description: Code-level vulnerabilities within the authentication process allowing privilege escalation.
threats.mitigation: Continuous vulnerability scanning and penetration testing.

threats.title: E2 - Privilege Escalation via Server Vulnerabilities
threats.description: Attacker exploits server flaws to bypass authorization checks.
threats.mitigation: Enforce principle of least privilege and strict role-based access control (RBAC).

threats.title: T - Tampering with authorization logic
threats.description: Malicious modification of internal memory or configuration altering permissions.
threats.mitigation: Use signed and immutable configuration files, memory protection.

threats.title: E2.1 - The server has cross-site scripting vulnerabilities
threats.description: XSS vulnerabilities on the server side of the login page.
threats.mitigation: Sanitize and encode all outputs, use Content Security Policy (CSP).

threats.title: T - Tampering with Data
threats.description: An attacker altering or deleting logs to cover their tracks.

threats.title: R1.2 - Log record is not perfect
threats.description: No log records or inadequate log records, allowing users to deny actions.

threats.title: I - Information Disclosure of Logs
threats.description: Unauthorized access to logs revealing sensitive system or user information.
threats.mitigation: Encrypt logs at rest and restrict access.

threats.title: T - Account Data Tampering
threats.description: An attacker altering account balances or permissions directly in the database.
threats.mitigation: Strict access control, database activity monitoring, and encryption.

threats.title: I - Exposure of Account Information
threats.description: A database breach exposing all user credentials and PII.
threats.mitigation: Encrypt sensitive fields at rest and use robust hashing (e.g., Argon2) for passwords.

threats.title: D - Database Overload
threats.description: Overloading the database so legitimate authentications cannot read account data.
threats.mitigation: Database clustering, query optimization, and connection pooling.

threats.title: S1 - Counterfeit other user identity
threats.description: Illegally obtained certificate, unsecure certification, passwords be cracked, brute force, session mechanism is not perfect.

threats.title: S2 - Counterfeit client identity communication
threats.description: Malware simulate keyboard actions, simulate client sending message, or fake user's operation.

threats.title: T1 - Client is embedded malicious program
threats.description: Malware tampered the user request data, modify browser memory, modify message sent/received.

threats.title: R1 - Users deny carried out transactions
threats.description: Lack of transaction signature mechanism.

threats.title: I1 - Malware to steal user sensitive information
threats.description: Malware to steal passwords via keyboard record, screenshots, or browser memory.

threats.title: I2 - Sensitive information without secure handling
threats.description: Sensitive info stored locally, encryption key on client, temporary files not deleted.
threats.mitigation: Prevent local caching of sensitive data.

threats.title: I3 - Fake site to cheat user input
threats.description: User is fished (Phishing).

threats.title: E1 - Client security vulnerabilities
threats.description: Client control vulnerabilities, kernel driver flaws, components leading to remote attack, cross-domain attack.

threats.title: S1 - B2B/B2C is a fraud site
threats.description: Server site to URL, Domain spoofing, Content spoofing, Framework embedded, ARP spoofing.

threats.title: S2 - B2B/B2C is a fake site
threats.description: Illegally obtained certificate or B2B/B2C authentication is not secure.

threats.title: T1 - B2B/B2C site is embedded malware
threats.description: Malware alters transaction requests coming from the partner.

threats.title: T2 - B2B/B2C site is controlled by attacker
threats.description: Compromised partner sends malicious traffic.

threats.title: R1 - B2B/B2C business party deny carried out transaction
threats.description: No valid signature or log record is not perfect.

threats.title: E2 - The server security vulnerabilities
threats.description: The server has cross-site scripting or other vulnerabilities allowing privilege escalation.

threats.title: E2 - The server security vulnerabilities
threats.description: Vulnerabilities in the payment processing logic.

threats.title: E2 - The server security vulnerabilities
threats.description: Vulnerabilities in client authentication logic.

threats.title: I4 - Security mechanism is not perfect
threats.description: Weak or missing channel security exposes identification confirmation.

threats.title: D1 - Channel overload
threats.description: Abnormal parameters or flood of confirmation requests.

threats.title: D2 - Undermine message integrity
threats.description: Compromised integrity of confirmation payload.

threats.title: D1 - Channel overload, leading log processing hang or crash
threats.description: Abnormal parameters lead to log processing crash or hang.

threats.title: R1.2 - Log record is not perfect
threats.description: No log records or inadequate records created during identification.

threats.title: I4 - Security mechanism is not perfect
threats.description: Weak or no channel security mechanism exposes the payment result to interception.

threats.title: D2 - Undermine message integrity
threats.description: Payment result message integrity is compromised.

threats.title: I4 - Security mechanism is not perfect
threats.description: No message security mechanism, weak message security, or no channel security.

threats.title: D1 - Channel overload
threats.description: Abnormal parameters lead to high CPU/memory consumption, multiple concurrent operations cause server to hang or crash, SYN/HTTP flood.

threats.title: D2 - Undermine message integrity
threats.description: Failure of message integrity causing processing errors.
